content:
Hey, for the Rust LSM I created, it would be great if I could get a quick sanity check if my usage of unsafe for the bindings looks correct. These are of course far from production ready, which is why I put review in quotes in the title. At the moment they only allow registering a single LSM directly, which is why I included them with my LSM code, even though it breaks the "only the kernel crate may access the bindings crate" rule. Long term there probably needs to be a macro, similar to how normal module registration works, if someone actually wants to upstream them. For my implementation the current state was enough and I didn't want to spend weeks of thesis time on refining the bindings. And while they do work, that is of course not an indication, that all unsafe is correct :)

I have a branch with only the bindings and a small demo LSM, that just allows every request, which I could use to send an RFC PATCH. Should I just send this to the list? And maybe also to the LSM list, then with a bit more explanation about it? Not sure if the latter is really necessary, since it is in a very early stage and I'm mainly looking for feedback if I fucked up some unsafe somewhere. Feels like it makes sense to include them though.
Side note: I'll try to join the open meeting on Wednesday, I think I missed the last one by about a week because I didn't yet know about it well enough when it happened.
The open meeting is likely to move to next week.
Due to a conflict with a meeting with Rust language team.
Ah damn, not sure if I have time next week, I might have to make some adjustments. I guess the final date will be posted somewhere as soon as it is clear?
Sorry, I lost trackings on this, should have sent it earlier.
Thank you, I'll see if I can make it.
I have sent the RFC PATCH for the bindings: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250416213206.26060-2-kernel@o1oo11oo.de/

I got some feedback from @Fiona Behrens personally at Easterhegg this weekend, the gist of it was that the unsafe use should be ok (from a quick glimpse), but what I created isn't really a proper abstraction yet and more of a direct use of whatever C does and of course needs some rethinking and restructuring (please correct me on what I misremembered).
Generally yeah. Didn’t look to much into the unsafe called due to not knowing the c api and having worked on some soldering at the same time :)
